root_text.an5.209:1.1: “Pañcime, bhikkhave, ādīnavā āyatakena gītassarena dhammaṁ bhaṇantassa.
root_text.an5.209:1.3: Attanāpi tasmiṁ sare sārajjati, parepi tasmiṁ sare sārajjanti, gahapatikāpi ujjhāyanti:
root_text.an5.209:1.4: ‘yatheva mayaṁ gāyāma, evamevaṁ kho samaṇā sakyaputtiyā gāyantī’ti, sarakuttimpi nikāmayamānassa samādhissa bhaṅgo hoti, pacchimā janatā diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.209:1.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ādīnavā āyatakena gītassarena dhammaṁ bhaṇantassā”ti.

translation_text.an5.209:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five drawbacks in reciting with a drawn-out singing voice.
translation_text.an5.209:1.3: You relish the sound of your own voice. Others relish the sound of your voice. Householders complain:
translation_text.an5.209:1.4: ‘These ascetics who follow the Sakyan, sing just like us!’ When you’re enjoying the melody, your immersion breaks up. Those who come after follow your example.
translation_text.an5.209:1.5: These are the five drawbacks in reciting with a drawn-out singing voice.”
